rulings.Case Title: DIOSDADO FACTOR JR. VS. MERCY HOUSING CORP. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 7, 2009, line 3. DEFENDANT MERCY SERVICES CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifts the burden by showing plaintiff was terminated for a legitimate business decision. (Plaintiff's failure to respond to the emergency on June 19, 2006 and the workplace problems described in warning #2 dated May 30, 2006, Termination letter dated June 21, 2006, UMF No. 13.) Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact, no specific or substantial evidence of discrimination, retaliation or harassment. In deposition, plaintiff did not testify that any discrimination was mentioned by his supervisors. (UMF 18-20, 24-25.) Plaintiff's letter of May 30, 2006 wherein he complains that he wasn't properly paid, viewed in the light most favorable to plaintiff, does not create a triable issue of fact on any of the claims, as plaintiff is merely stating that in his opinion, Camel withheld pay. Plaintiff's personal suspicions of improper motive based on conjecture or speculation are insufficient. Plaintiff's opinion alone, without more, does not create a triable issue of fact. (See Kerr v. Rose (1990) 216 Cal. App. 3d 1551, 1563-1564.) Defendants objection to Factor declaration overruled as to #1, sustained as to #2. All of plaintiff's objections overruled. =(302/CWW)
